checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any fabricated paragraphs or sentences. All body text and headings in the JSON correlate directly to content found in the source PDF. Logical additions like the 'Name' header in the attendance table or descriptive captions are acceptable for accessibility.
There is no contact information (phone numbers, emails, URLs) present in the source document, and none has been fabricated in the output.
All pages (1-4) and all numbered agenda items (1-14) from the source document are represented in the output. While some sections are truncated (data loss), no entire page or major section is missing.

Multiple tables in the output are severely truncated. The 'Attendance' table on page 1 lists only 3 staff members (Amy Berenson, Rick Blumer, Chris Carlin) out of the 17 present in the source, representing a ~47% loss of total table rows. Additionally, the three vote tables (October 2021 Minutes, 2022 Meeting Dates, and 2022 Committee Goals) on pages 2 and 3 list only 2 members (Rachel Bello and Randy Cress) out of the 10 members listed in the source, representing an 80% loss of data rows per table.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source document's Attendance table lists 17 staff members (Staff section, page 1) and each vote table (pages 2-3) lists 10 voting members (Rachel Bello through Earl Struble).
checks.b_evidence_output: The output JSON 'Meeting Attendance Roster' table lists only 3 staff members. The 'October 2021 Meeting Minutes Approval Vote', '2022 Technology Committee Dates Vote', and '2022 Technology Committee Goals Vote' tables each list only 2 members.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections: 'Attendance' (index 2) and 'Agenda Items' (vote tables under headings 4, 5, and 6).

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The truncation of tables mentioned in C4 leads to significant information loss within several sections. In the 'Attendance' section, the omission of 14 staff members constitutes a loss of approximately 50% of the section's content. In the 'Agenda Items' section, the omission of 80% of the voting data in three subsections represents a major loss of fidelity regarding the meeting's official actions.
checks.b_evidence_source: Complete lists of staff attendance (17 names) and member votes (10 names per vote) on pages 1, 2, and 3.
checks.b_evidence_output: Truncated tables containing only a fraction of the original data (3 staff names and 2 voting names per table).

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text that was successfully converted is highly accurate. Numbers, names, and dates match the source document precisely. The splitting of Item 14 into a heading and a paragraph is considered acceptable reformatting.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output against the source document and found no instances of duplicated paragraphs, sections, or list items that were not present in the original material.

The heading hierarchy is logically sound. The main agenda items (1-14) are correctly assigned level 2 headings, and the subsection 'Minutes Vote' is assigned a level 3 heading. While the document title is not explicitly tagged as an H1 in the elements, it is present in the metadata, which is an acceptable normalization.

All tables in the document are severely truncated and structurally corrupted. In the Attendance table (Page 1), only the first 3 of 17 staff members are included. In all vote tables (Pages 2-3), the source contains 10 rows for members, but the output only includes 2 members. Furthermore, the first data row (Rachel Bello) is incorrectly identified as the header row in the vote tables.
checks.b_evidence_source: Attendance table on Page 1 lists staff members from Amy Berenson down to Troy Whitefield. Vote tables on Pages 2 and 3 list 10 members (Rachel Bello to Earl Struble).
checks.b_evidence_output: The Attendance table ends after 'Chris Carlin'. Vote tables (e.g., 'October 2021 Meeting Minutes Approval Vote') only contain 'Rachel Bello' and 'Randy Cress', with the former marked as cell_type: 'header'.

checks.a_analysis: The information flows logically and follows the chronological/top-to-bottom order established in the source PDF.

Under item 6, '2022 Technology Committee Goals', the source PDF presents five distinct goals (Goal 1A through Goal 4) as a list. The output renders these as separate paragraphs rather than a semantic list structure.
checks.b_evidence_source: Goal 1A: Develop CAD... Goal 1B: Develop CAD... Goal 2: Develop plan... Goal 3: Address technical... Goal 4: Consider framework...
checks.b_evidence_output: Five separate paragraph elements following the text 'The goals were voted on:'

checks.check_name: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
checks.a_analysis: There is a significant mismatch between the headings in the semantic JSON (15) and the rendered HTML (19). The difference of 4 headings exceeds the allowable threshold of 10% or 3 headings, indicating the renderer added headings not defined in the source's logical structure.
The source document does not contain hyperlinks, and the output correctly reflects this by containing no fabricated or injected links.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the document is severely compromised by systemic truncation of content within tables. Major sections that rely on tabular data (Attendance and several Vote sections) have had their visual footprints drastically reduced, showing only a fraction of the original information. This significantly alters the density and flow of the document compared to the source PDF, where these tables are prominent and complete multi-page features.
checks.b_evidence_source: Full tables for Attendance (27 entries) and multiple Votes (10 members each) occupying significant vertical space on pages 1, 2, and 3.
checks.b_evidence_output: Tables for Attendance are truncated after 13 rows, and all Vote tables are truncated to just 2 rows, fundamentally changing the layout and visual content of those sections.
checks.b_evidence_location: Attendance section and Agenda Items 4, 5, and 6

checks.a_analysis: Every table in the converted document is visually misrendered due to missing rows. The Attendance table stops after 'Chris Carlin', omitting 14 staff members. The 'Minutes Vote', 'Dates Vote', and 'Goals Vote' tables all stop after the second member, 'Randy Cress', omitting 8 committee members from each table. This is a systemic rendering failure.
checks.b_evidence_source: Attendance table on Page 1 contains 10 members and 17 staff members. Vote tables on pages 2 and 3 contain 10 members each.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML Attendance table ends after 'Chris Carlin'. The 'October 2021 Meeting Minutes Approval Vote', '2022 Technology Committee Dates Vote', and '2022 Technology Committee Goals Vote' tables only show 'Rachel Bello' and 'Randy Cress'.

checks.a_analysis: The primary 'NC DIT 911 Board' logo from the source PDF header is rendered as a solid black rectangle in the HTML output, and the secondary logo at the very top of the PDF is missing entirely.
checks.b_evidence_source: NC DIT 911 Board logo appearing at the top left of the page and inside the header box on page 1.
checks.b_evidence_output: A solid black rectangle appears in the 'Document Header' section where the logo should be, and the top-most logo is absent.

checks.a_analysis: No visual content duplication was observed in the rendered HTML screenshot.
While the document is readable in parts, the massive loss of data in tables and the broken header image significantly degrade the professional quality and utility of the document.
